close_reason: Implemented in 3bf620ec: replaced the null MermaidSourceRender component with useMermaidSourceRender, preserved stale-result protection, and centralized dependency-aware lifecycle semantics.
description:
## Background
The no-useEffect migration introduced `MermaidSourceRender` in `src/components/mermaid/MermaidDiagram.tsx`. It returns `null`, performs the asynchronous `renderMermaid` lifecycle solely to call parent setters, and is keyed only to manufacture a remount boundary. This is a shallow React component seam: it has no UI and delegates all presentation state back to `MermaidDiagram`.

## Evaluation and plan
Replace the null-returning component with a purpose-built `useMermaidSourceRender` hook. The hook will own the asynchronous Mermaid request and its active/unmounted stale-result guard; its small interface will report successful SVG output or a normalized error to the presentation component. Use the dependency-aware external-lifecycle helper with `[source, onRendered, onError]` so a source change is a real reactive rebind rather than an implicit mount-only assumption. Memoize the parent result callbacks so the hook does not restart for ordinary renders.

Keep the visible tab, loading, error, source, and SVG viewport UI in `MermaidDiagram`. Keep the keyed `MermaidDiagramViewport` mount boundary because it owns the imperative DOM/Panzoom lifecycle. Remove the `MermaidSourceRender` key and component entirely; source changes should reset render state synchronously and let the hook restart from the current source.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No null-returning `MermaidSourceRender` component remains.
- [ ] Mermaid source rendering is encapsulated by a named custom hook with an explicit source/callback interface.
- [ ] Every source renders, successful output selects Diagram, and failures select Source with the complete error message.
- [ ] A stale render cannot update state after a source change or unmount.
- [ ] Existing SVG mounting, Panzoom controls, cleanup, and source-change behavior remain intact.
- [ ] Mermaid component tests cover success, failure, stale completion, unmount completion, and Panzoom cleanup.
- [ ] Typecheck, lint, and focused tests pass.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/components/mermaid/MermaidDiagram.test.tsx`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check`

Triage: planned for implementation on `bsm-gbd/eliminate-direct-useeffect`.
title: Replace Mermaid source lifecycle middleman with a render hook
